original_question:
Given three red beads, two white beads, and one blue bead are placed in line in random order, determine the probability that no two neighboring beads are the same color.

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}

similar_questions.question: Given four green beads, three yellow beads, and two purple beads are placed in line in random order, determine the probability that no two neighboring beads are the same color.
Given five orange beads, one pink bead, and three black beads are placed in line in random order, determine the probability that no two neighboring beads are the same color.
Given two blue beads, four green beads, and three red beads are placed in line in random order, determine the probability that no two neighboring beads are the same color.
Given six pink beads, two turquoise beads, and two silver beads are placed in line in random order, determine the probability that no two neighboring beads are the same color.
Given three gray beads, three white beads, and four black beads are placed in line in random order, determine the probability that no two neighboring beads are the same color.
